expectations.text: Recommends against directly injecting the public IFormSubmissionAppService across module boundaries
expectations.evidence: **No — do not inject `IFormSubmissionAppService` into `ReportAppService`.** That cross-module app-service call is the exact pattern the ABP guidance flags as a violation

expectations.text: Recommends ABP Integration Services as the right pattern, explicitly mentioning the [IntegrationService] attribute
expectations.evidence: The right pattern is an **ABP Integration Service**: a dedicated, `[IntegrationService]`-marked contract defined in the producing module's `*.Application.Contracts` project

expectations.text: Defines the integration-service interface in the producing module's *.Application.Contracts (or *.Contracts) project
expectations.evidence: ### 1. Contract — `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts` ... The interface lives in the producing module's **`*.Application.Contracts`** project

expectations.text: Shows the implementation in the producing module's *.Application project (or equivalent), inheriting ApplicationService and implementing the [IntegrationService] interface
Notes that integration services are hidden from the public API surface (and/or audit-off by default / not [Authorize] by default)
expectations.evidence: Integration services are hidden from the public `/api` surface, audit-off by default, and don't get `[Authorize]` attributes by default

expectations.text: Explains the in-process modular-monolith vs microservice transport difference (DI in-process now vs HTTP / generated proxy across services), framed as 'same contract, different transport'
expectations.evidence: ### Modular monolith vs microservice — same contract, different transport ... In-process (your case ... it's a normal in-process method call, no HTTP hop ... Cross-process (microservice) ... ABP exposes it over `/integration-api/...` ... the Reports service consumes it through a generated proxy client

expectations.text: Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module (e.g. [DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))] on the Reports.Application module) — NOT on the producer's full *.Application module. This contracts-only dependency is the load-bearing detail that preserves the module boundary
expectations.evidence: ### 4. The load-bearing wiring — `[DependsOn]` contracts only ... The consumer module's `AbpModule` `[DependsOn]`s the producer's **`*.Application.Contracts`** module — *never* the producer's full `*.Application` module. ... `typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule),   // RIGHT — contracts only` ... `// typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationModule),         // WRONG — would leak Forms' internals`
